content:
F O R T U N E • T H E B R I E F abundant buffet of giant squid the size of Mini Coopers. I ask Armour the depth. “Fifteen hundred meters,” he replies: nearly 5,000 feet. I’m ready neither for daddy, nor to swim in that. Adrenaline doesn’t care what you’re ready for. “Let’s go! Let’s go!” someone shouts from the front of the tender. “Group two in the water—now!” I mash my feet into fins, strap on a mask, and plop into the two-foot swells behind Armour. Science-based ecotour- ism has traditionally been a more rugged affair,

FORTUNE PRESIDENT TRUMP CAN’T BELIEVE Jensen Huang doesn’t own his own plane. Hours before he departs for his highly anticipated China summit, the president has been arranging for the billionaire cofounder of Nvidia PRESIDENT	to join the who’s who of Fortune 500 CEOs preparing to travel to Beijing. Also in the group are Citigroup’s Jane Fraser, whomFortunejust dubbed DONALD	the world’s Most Powerful Woman in Business, and Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg, who recently gave the presi- dent an honorary (if slightly tongue- in-cheek) “Salesman of the Year” TRUMP award for helping the jetmaker sell hundreds of planes. Huang is a late but welcome addi- tion to the party. For one of America’s AN HOUR most successful CEOs and the man whose company’s chips power the AI boom, the president is happy to make room, and Huang winds up hitching IN THE	a ride aboard Air Force One, shar- ing the jet with Elon Musk, among others. The only reason Huang wasn’t included earlier is because he didn’t OVAL OFFICE	call to ask. As I sit across the Resolute Desk from the president in the Oval Office while we talk about his upcoming WITH	trip, it’s clear that arrangements like this are exactly the kind of deal the president likes to make—quick, informal, and one in which he can declare himself a clear winner. He AMERICA’S prides himself on his ability to get anyone on the phone and achieve measurable results, whether he’s talking with a world leader or an CEO-IN-CHIEF American company he wants to help. In a wide-ranging conversation that spanned an hour—and covered topics from tariffs to AI data centers to the war in Iran—the president outlined the broader, top-down dealmaking mentality he’s using to try to reinvigorate the American BY ALYSON SHONTELL	economy.
